mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS showed a strong print of +227k for Jan 2017, continuing a multi-year expansion.
failure_condition: A strategy going long S&P 500 when the 6-month momentum of PAYEMS is positive fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sudden increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a leading indicator of recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and causing negative equity returns as investors anticipate a broad economic contraction.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of instances where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflationary environment, characterized by accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) concurrent with decelerating industrial production (INDPRO), severely compresses corporate profit margins and increases discount rates, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflationary period was poor for equities.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal does not remain statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is actively tightening policy in a high-rate environment, further increases in the FEDFUNDS rate act as a strong headwind for equity valuations by increasing the risk-free rate used for discounting future cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed had begun its tightening cycle in Dec 2015 and signaled more hikes.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is above 2.5%, the correlation between monthly changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise or sharp deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which is a primary driver of the US economy. This signal prompts a rapid downward revision of near-term earnings forecasts, causing negative short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward return following a negative monthly RSAFS print is not statistically different from the mean return following a positive print (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and industrial sector, signaling robust business investment and output. This strength translates to higher aggregate corporate revenues and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO 3-month momentum and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above the central bank's implicit target, it forces contractionary monetary policy, raising the discount rate for equities and eroding the real value of future earnings, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Jan 2017, YoY CPI was 2.5%, approaching levels that could trigger a more hawkish Fed response.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is not statistically lower in periods where YoY CPI > 3% compared to periods where it is < 3%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market (low UNRATE) and slowing real demand (decelerating RSAFS) signals a late-cycle peak. This indicates that while employment is maxed out, consumer capacity to spend is waning, presaging an economic downturn and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:In Feb 2017, UNRATE was low (4.8%) while some questioned the sustainability of demand growth.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative 60-day returns) is less than 55% over a full business cycle.
